details: begin numerical stability analysis of multigammaln_lanczos
read multigammaln_lanczos_program_descriptor.py (HiFi4 + fp32_dest_acc_en=True, all CBs float32, no UnpackToDestMode set, no math_approx_mode set)
read kernels/multigammaln_lanczos_compute.cpp (per-tile 4xLanczos-6 with cb_accumulator round-trip and unary_ne pole mask on a)
read kernels/multigammaln_lanczos_reader.cpp (streaming reader, 1 push per tile, compute reads tile 4x without popping)
confirmed math_approx_mode defaults to false in Python ComputeConfigDescriptor binding (program_descriptors.cpp:618) — recip_tile/log_tile run in PRECISE mode
identified per-tile accumulation chain: 4 lgamma evaluations -> 4 fp32 round-trips through cb_accumulator (Float32 CB, but UnpackToDestMode=Default, NOT UnpackToDestFp32)
identified pole mask applied to 'a' (D2) not 'result' (D0), avoiding NaN*0=NaN; verified by reading kernel lines 167-179
counted Lanczos error sources per lgamma: 6 recip + 6 mul_unary scalar + 6 add_binary, plus 2 log_tile, 1 sub of similar-magnitude (catastrophic cancellation candidate at log(temp) - (a + 3.581))
identified key observation: op is SFPU-only — math_fidelity HiFi4 setting has no effect; math_approx_mode is the real precision lever (correctly at false)
identified one-line improvement: setting unpack_to_dest_mode[cb_accumulator]=UnpackToDestFp32 would close the TF32 truncation edge on reload
